complete_command_examples.overview: Complete end-to-end examples of creating slash commands for different scenarios and use cases. Each example includes the full workflow from requirements to implementation.

example.name: api_endpoint_generator
example.scenario: Create a command that helps developers generate REST API endpoints with all the necessary boilerplate and best practices.

requirements.requirement: Command should accept endpoint name and HTTP method as arguments
Should switch to code mode for file creation
Include security, testing, and documentation requirements
Should be available to entire team

implementation.content:
--- description: Generate REST API endpoint with best practices argument-hint: <endpoint-name> <http-method> mode: code ---
Create a REST API endpoint with the following specifications:
**Core Implementation:** - Route handler with proper HTTP method - Request validation using Joi - Error handling with appropriate status codes - Response formatting following our API standards
**Security:** - Authentication middleware - Authorization checks - Input sanitization - Rate limiting consideration
**Documentation:** - OpenAPI/Swagger annotations - Example requests and responses - Error response documentation
**Testing:** - Unit tests for business logic - Integration tests for the endpoint - Test error scenarios
Follow our project's REST API conventions and Express.js patterns.

usage.step: Roo switches to code mode and creates the endpoint

example.name: security_review_command
example.scenario: Create a command for performing comprehensive security reviews of code, focusing on common vulnerabilities.

requirements.requirement: No arguments needed - works with selected code
Can be used in any mode

implementation.content:
--- description: Comprehensive security analysis and vulnerability detection ---
Perform a thorough security review of the selected code:
**Authentication & Authorization:** - Verify proper authentication checks - Check authorization before sensitive operations - Review session management - Validate token handling
**Input Validation:** - Identify potential injection points (SQL, XSS, LDAP, Command) - Check input sanitization and escaping - Verify data type validation - Review file upload handling and restrictions
**Data Security:** - Check for hardcoded secrets or credentials - Verify encryption for sensitive data at rest and in transit - Review secure communication protocols (HTTPS, TLS) - Check password hashing methods (bcrypt, Argon2) - Validate secure random number generation
**Common Vulnerabilities:** - OWASP Top 10 considerations - Check dependencies for known vulnerabilities - Review error messages for information leakage - Check for insecure deserialization - Verify CSRF protection - Check for clickjacking protections
**Access Control:** - Review principle of least privilege implementation - Check for horizontal/vertical privilege escalation - Verify resource-level authorization
**Output Encoding:** - Check for proper output encoding - Verify context-appropriate escaping - Review templating security
For each finding, provide: - Severity level (Critical/High/Medium/Low) - Specific code location - Explanation of the vulnerability - Concrete remediation steps - Example of secure implementation

example.name: test_generator_command
example.scenario: Create a command that generates comprehensive test suites for selected code, including unit and integration tests.

requirements.requirement: Generate tests in project's testing framework
Create files, so needs code mode

implementation.content:
--- description: Generate comprehensive test suites for selected code mode: code ---
Create a complete test suite for the selected code:
**Unit Tests:** - Test all public methods/functions - Cover happy path scenarios - Test edge cases and boundary conditions - Test error handling paths - Mock external dependencies - Aim for 90%+ code coverage
**Test Organization:** - Group related tests with describe blocks - Use clear, descriptive test names: "it should... when..." - Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) - Keep tests independent and isolated - One assertion per test when possible
**Test Data:** - Use meaningful, realistic test data - Create reusable fixtures when appropriate - Test with valid and invalid inputs - Include boundary values
**Assertions:** - Verify return values match expectations - Check state changes are correct - Validate side effects occur as expected - Test error messages are appropriate
**Integration Tests:** - Test component interactions - Verify database operations - Test API endpoints with real requests - Check external service integrations
Use Jest and React Testing Library following our project conventions. Include setup and teardown functions where needed.

usage.step: Select function or component to test
Roo switches to code mode and creates test file

example.name: architecture_review_command
example.scenario: Create a command that performs architectural analysis and provides improvement recommendations, using architect mode capabilities.

requirements.requirement: Needs architect mode for planning focus

implementation.content:
--- description: Analyze system architecture and propose improvements mode: architect ---
Review the current system architecture and provide recommendations:
**Architecture Analysis:** 1. **Component Structure** - Document component hierarchy and relationships - Identify dependencies and data flow - Map service boundaries
2. **Design Patterns** - Identify patterns currently in use - Evaluate pattern appropriateness - Suggest additional beneficial patterns
3. **Coupling and Cohesion** - Measure coupling between components - Assess cohesion within modules - Identify tight coupling issues - Find components with weak cohesion
4. **Scalability Assessment** - Identify bottlenecks - Review stateless vs stateful components - Assess horizontal scaling readiness - Check for single points of failure
**Architectural Concerns:** - Data consistency strategies - Error handling and recovery - Security boundaries - Performance considerations - Deployment complexity
**Recommendations:** For each issue, provide: - **Priority**: Critical/High/Medium/Low - **Current State**: What exists now - **Proposed Solution**: Specific architectural change - **Benefits**: Expected improvements - **Risks**: Potential challenges - **Implementation**: High-level approach
**Deliverables:** - Architecture diagram (ASCII or description) - Component relationship map - Prioritized improvement roadmap - Quick wins vs long-term initiatives

usage.step: Navigate to project or select files
Roo switches to architect mode and performs analysis

example.name: component_creator_with_args
example.scenario: Create a command that generates React components with proper structure, accepting the component name as an argument.

requirements.requirement: Create multiple files (component, styles, tests, story)
Needs code mode for file creation

implementation.content:
--- description: Create a new React component with all supporting files argument-hint: <component-name> mode: code ---
Create a new React component with the specified name:
**Component File (ComponentName.tsx):** - Functional component using TypeScript - Proper prop interface with JSDoc comments - Exported component and prop types - Display name set for debugging - Default props where appropriate
**Styling (ComponentName.module.css):** - CSS Module for scoped styling - Use CSS custom properties for theming - Follow our BEM naming within component - Responsive design considerations
**Storybook Story (ComponentName.stories.tsx):** - Default export with component metadata - At least 3 stories: Default, With Props, Edge Cases - Controls for interactive prop testing - Accessibility annotations
**Unit Tests (ComponentName.test.tsx):** - Test rendering with minimal props - Test all prop combinations - Test user interactions - Test accessibility with @testing-library/jest-dom - Aim for 100% coverage
**Structure:** Place all files in: `src/components/ComponentName/`
Follow our project's component conventions: - Use named exports for components - Include PropTypes for runtime validation - Use semantic HTML elements - Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance

usage.step: Roo creates all files in proper structure

example.name: refactoring_command
example.scenario: Create a command that identifies refactoring opportunities and suggests improvements for selected code.

requirements.requirement: Can suggest changes without making them

implementation.content:
--- description: Identify and suggest refactoring opportunities ---
Analyze the selected code for refactoring opportunities:
**Code Smells:** - Long methods (>20 lines) - Long parameter lists (>4 parameters) - Duplicate code - Large classes (>300 lines) - Feature envy (method more interested in other class) - Data clumps (groups of data appearing together) - Primitive obsession - God objects with too many responsibilities
**SOLID Principles:** - **Single Responsibility**: Check if classes/functions do one thing - **Open/Closed**: Can extend without modifying? - **Liskov Substitution**: Subtypes properly substitutable? - **Interface Segregation**: Are interfaces too large? - **Dependency Inversion**: Depend on abstractions?
**Refactoring Suggestions:** For each opportunity, provide:
1. **Current Issue**: What's wrong 2. **Refactoring Type**: Extract method, introduce parameter object, etc. 3. **Proposed Solution**: How to refactor 4. **Benefits**: Why it's worth doing 5. **Code Example**: Before and after snippets
**Naming Improvements:** - Variable names too short or unclear - Function names not descriptive - Class names not representing purpose - Suggest better names
**Complexity Reduction:** - Simplify nested conditionals - Replace switch statements with polymorphism - Reduce cyclomatic complexity - Extract magic numbers to constants
**Design Patterns:** Suggest where patterns could improve code: - Strategy pattern for varying algorithms - Factory pattern for object creation - Observer pattern for event handling - Decorator pattern for extending behavior
Prioritize suggestions by impact and effort required.

example.name: multi_step_deployment_check
example.scenario: Create a command that guides through pre-deployment verification steps, ensuring nothing is missed before deploying.

implementation.content:
--- description: Comprehensive pre-deployment verification checklist ---
Perform a complete deployment readiness check:
**Phase 1: Code Quality** - [ ] All tests passing (unit, integration, E2E) - [ ] No linting errors or warnings - [ ] Code coverage meets threshold (>80%) - [ ] No TypeScript/type errors - [ ] Run security audit: `npm audit` or equivalent - [ ] Check for outdated dependencies with security issues
**Phase 2: Configuration** - [ ] Environment variables documented in .env.example - [ ] Secrets configured in deployment environment - [ ] Database connection strings verified - [ ] API keys for third-party services configured - [ ] Feature flags set appropriately - [ ] CORS settings configured for production
**Phase 3: Database** - [ ] All migrations tested and ready - [ ] Migration rollback tested - [ ] Database backup created - [ ] Data seeding scripts ready (if needed) - [ ] Database indexes optimized
**Phase 4: Documentation** - [ ] README.md up to date - [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated with version changes - [ ] API documentation current - [ ] Deployment guide reviewed - [ ] Runbook updated with new procedures
**Phase 5: Monitoring & Logging** - [ ] Error tracking configured (Sentry, etc.) - [ ] Logging levels appropriate - [ ] Performance monitoring setup - [ ] Health check endpoint working - [ ] Alerts configured for critical issues
**Phase 6: Performance** - [ ] Build optimization complete - [ ] Static assets cached properly - [ ] Images optimized - [ ] Lazy loading implemented where beneficial - [ ] Bundle size within acceptable limits
**Phase 7: Security** - [ ] Security headers configured - [ ] SSL/TLS certificate valid - [ ] Authentication/authorization tested - [ ] Rate limiting configured - [ ] Input validation reviewed
**Phase 8: Rollback Plan** - [ ] Previous version tagged in git - [ ] Rollback procedure documented and tested - [ ] Database rollback plan ready - [ ] Communication plan for rollback
**Phase 9: Communication** - [ ] Stakeholders notified of deployment - [ ] Maintenance window scheduled (if needed) - [ ] Team members aware and available - [ ] Status page updated
**Phase 10: Post-Deployment** - [ ] Smoke tests defined - [ ] Monitoring dashboards ready to observe - [ ] Team ready to respond to issues
Verify each item and report any failures or concerns before proceeding.

practice.name: clear_descriptions
practice.explanation: Every command has a concise, action-oriented description

practice.name: argument_hints
practice.explanation: Commands that need input show clear argument hints

practice.name: mode_targeting
practice.explanation: Commands use mode field when specific capabilities needed

practice.name: structured_content
practice.explanation: Commands use lists, sections, and clear organization

practice.name: project_context
practice.explanation: Commands reference project conventions and tools

practice.name: scope_appropriateness
practice.explanation: Team commands in project, personal commands global

customization_notes.note: Replace framework-specific references (Jest, React, Express) with your project's actual technology stack
Update file paths and structures to match your project conventions
Adjust coverage thresholds and quality metrics to your standards
Reference your actual tools (linters, test runners, build systems)
Include your team's specific coding standards and patterns
